exploitation_path: Two clients connect concurrently to stateless HTTP endpoints
A single shared McpServer instance is reused across transports
Transport/server state can be overwritten across requests
One client can receive another client's response data
validated_findings.recommendation: Create a fresh MCP server per request/session and upgrade the MCP SDK to a patched version.

validated_findings.vulnerability_type: Predictable OAuth state, auth codes, and client secrets
exploitation_path: External clients initiate OAuth flows or register dynamic clients
State, auth codes, or client secrets are generated with Math.random()
An attacker predicts or brute-forces these values
OAuth flow integrity or client identity can be subverted
validated_findings.recommendation: Use cryptographically secure randomness for all security-sensitive token material.

validated_findings.vulnerability_type: Plaintext refresh token exposure in bearer token payloads
exploitation_path: OAuth callback issues a signed bearer token containing the QuickBooks refresh token
Any holder of the bearer token can decode the JWT payload without the server secret
The embedded refresh token can be extracted for longer-lived QuickBooks access
validated_findings.recommendation: Encrypt bearer tokens or replace embedded credentials with server-side indirection.

validated_findings.vulnerability_type: Missing host-header validation for MCP HTTP endpoints
exploitation_path: A malicious site targets a locally reachable or reverse-proxied MCP HTTP endpoint
The server accepts arbitrary Host headers
Browser requests can be routed across a DNS rebinding boundary to the MCP server
validated_findings.recommendation: Apply an explicit host allow-list and upgrade to a patched MCP SDK release.

validated_findings.vulnerability_type: Callback helper accepted OAuth responses without mandatory state configuration
exploitation_path: The local callback helper is started without QUICKBOOKS_OAUTH_STATE
A forged or swapped authorization response is delivered to /callback
The helper processes the callback without enforcing state integrity
validated_findings.recommendation: Require QUICKBOOKS_OAUTH_STATE and reject callbacks whose state does not match exactly.

false_positives.reason: The remaining node-quickbooks -> underscore advisory requires deeply nested attacker-controlled arrays reaching _.flatten/_.isEqual. Exposed search-tool schemas constrain criteria to flat record objects or arrays, so this was not validated as an externally reachable runtime issue in this repository.
